reasoning: France are the clear market and performance leader, having reached three straight semifinals while outscoring opponents 16-2 and clearing every knockout tie without conceding, with Mbappé, Dembélé and Olise all in scoring form. They face Spain in a Dallas semifinal, so one elite side is eliminated before the final, leaving Argentina vs England on the other half; I favor the resilient, tournament-tested Argentina despite two nervy knockout wins over Egypt and Switzerland. Spain's balanced possession game and England's improved form (a gutsy 3-2 win at altitude in Mexico City) make both live threats to flip this pick. Norway is the dark horse: Haaland is on fire and they already dumped out Brazil, but they must get past England to matter. Confidence is Medium because the four remaining sides are tightly priced and knockout football at neutral, sometimes hot venues adds volatility.
